reasoning: Gibraltar is an extremely safe, small British Overseas Territory with a population of ~33,000. The baseline event count over the past year is exactly zero. For the resolution criteria to be met, more than ten events of this extraordinarily rare and specific type would miraculously occur in a single 30-day window out of nowhere. Recent news indicates a post-Brexit border agreement has been reached between the UK and EU, eliminating border tensions, and there are absolutely zero reports of organized armed groups operating in Gibraltar, let alone carrying out systemic violence against civilians. The chance of over ten such events occurring in a single month by January or February 2026 is practically negligible, effectively matching the baseline odds of a truly unprecedented, chaotic disaster hitting the microstate without any forewarning. I will give a 2% probability to both resolution dates to account for the possibility of unpredictable outlier scenarios or bizarre ACLED data-entry/coding errors, but the real probability is likely even closer to zero.
